title: Possibility Lattice Node
description: A floating junction where unrealized timelines intersect. The node is built from nested non-coplanar rings, a crystalline probability core, directional branch sockets, and orbiting decision markers. It must feel mathematically precise from a distance while revealing impossible spatial relationships and subtle instability up close.
narrativeFunction: The lattice node gives Parallel Remembrance a navigable topology. Nodes connect alternate outcomes, frame the destination's major regions, carry animated probability flow, and provide attachment points for bridges, archive conduits, garden threads, and drifting timeline fragments.

orientation.originMeaning: The origin is the center of the probability core and the intersection of all branch vectors. Ring animation, network placement, and focus behavior use this exact point.

silhouette.primary: A compact spherical knot made from four large rings at conflicting orientations surrounding one bright faceted core.
silhouette.secondary: Six branch arms terminate in small socket halos and create an uneven star-like extension around the central knot.
silhouette.tertiary: Decision markers, probability ticks, orbiting splinters, vector arrows, interference bands, and small crossing clamps gather near ring intersections.
silhouette.negativeSpace: Open windows between rings are fundamental. The core occupies less than one quarter of the node diameter, leaving multiple visible routes through the object.
silhouette.recognitionTest: At distance the object reads as a dimensional junction with nested rings and directional branches, not as a decorative atom, ordinary gyroscope, spherical portal, or simple star.

materials.surfaceDescription: Dark violet alloy with shifting anisotropic highlights, fine coordinate engravings, localized emission at intersections, and subtle color phase changes along each ring.
Translucent crystalline core with layered internal facets, bright caustic edges, cloudy density pockets, and changing internal depth rather than uniform glow.
Cool cyan-violet metal-glass rods with animated vector markings, brighter socket halos, translucent direction fins, and crisp engraved indices.

components.role: Four large rings defining incompatible coordinate planes.
components.detail: Each ring includes one discontinuity, unequal thickness near crossings, a unique coordinate engraving, and one brighter intersection segment.

components.role: Faceted central state volume and visual focus.
components.detail: Stretch selected facets into an irregular dodecahedral-like volume and preserve visible nested internal shells for changing state density.

components.role: Six directional network arms extending from the core cage.
components.detail: Arms taper away from the core, bend slightly near their tips, and contain one illuminated arrow indicating active probability flow.

components.role: Small luminous rings marking connection directions.
components.detail: Every halo has a broken segment facing away from the core, one vector tick, and a darker inactive state distinct from disconnected geometry.

components.role: Orbiting markers representing unresolved state choices.
components.detail: Markers vary in aspect ratio, carry one dim state glyph, and orbit on paths that do not match any of the four large rings.

assemblyOrder: Attach socket halos and vector markings
Distribute decision markers on independent orbits
deformationRules: No pair of dimensional rings may become coplanar or share the same major radius.
The probability core may breathe by four percent but remains centered at the origin.
Branch arms bend outward by up to eight degrees near sockets to avoid a perfectly radial star.
variationRules: Active connections brighten their branch arms and halos while inactive branches remain structurally visible but dim.
Decision-marker count ranges from eight to fourteen and orbit planes vary by node.
One ring discontinuity aligns approximately with the dominant outgoing branch but never exactly.

topology.target: Game-ready modular hard-surface and crystal topology with separable ring sections, independent branch sockets, a riggable core shell, and instanced decision markers.
topology.bevel: Use narrow ring and branch bevels, crisp halo edges, and softened irregular crystal facets that preserve the core's angular identity.
topology.subdivision: Use controlled subdivision only on the probability core's internal shells and selected ring transitions; keep decision markers deliberately faceted.
topology.normals: Weighted normals stabilize metal rings and branch arms while smooth crystal normals retain distinct facet breaks through geometry rather than shading artifacts.
topology.uv: Use directional ring UVs, one vector-mark trim, unique core refraction coordinates, and an emissive mask per branch state.

animation.motionCharacter: Rings rotate at incommensurate slow rates. The core changes internal density instead of simply scaling, and decision markers occasionally reverse as though a state has been reconsidered.
constraints: Branch sockets retain stable world-facing orientation.
Active flow reaches connected sockets without discontinuity.

collision.description: Use one simple spherical interaction volume centered on the probability core. Rings, branch arms, halos, and markers remain non-colliding.

sockets.purpose: Primary network connection toward archive structures.
Bridge or filament connection toward positive X.
Bridge or filament connection toward negative X.
Camera and interaction target at the probability core.

orthographicGuidance.front: Show four distinguishable ring orientations, six branch directions, open windows, and the bright core without excessive tangencies.
orthographicGuidance.side: Confirm equal depth complexity and prevent the node from collapsing into a flat emblem.
orthographicGuidance.top: Reveal branch hierarchy, ring discontinuities, and decision-marker orbits that differ from the main rings.
orthographicGuidance.threeQuarter: Use a slightly elevated angle showing the core through multiple ring windows and at least three socket halos projecting toward the viewer.
orthographicGuidance.scaleCue: Include a human one meter from the node; the complete knot is larger than a person but still approachable as an interactive junction.

validation: The node contains four clearly non-coplanar large rings and one central faceted core.
At least four open windows through the ring structure remain visible from hero view.
Six branch arms terminate in separately modeled broken socket halos.
Decision markers use independent orbit planes rather than following the main rings.
Active branch highlighting does not illuminate the complete node uniformly.
LOD2 preserves the core, two crossing ring directions, and three major branch vectors.
Focus and network sockets retain stable orientation while rings animate.
